ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Ka teŋɛ zaa ba wa de yɛ saanoŋ kyɛ zagre ka ba kyɛlle woŋ a yɛ kannoo, ka yɛnee wa yire a be yɛ piŋ a yɛ gbɛɛ uuruŋ bare ka a e danseɛ do ba.”
translated: And all the things that we are doing now, if we had done them in the past, we would have been rich, but we are just sitting idle and letting poverty take over our homes.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: A Naa Hɛrɔde naaloŋ saŋ, ba naŋ da dɔge a Yeezu Bɛtelɛhɛm naŋ be Gyudiya paaloŋ poɔ, a lɛ puoriŋ, yɛmbaŋnema mine da yi la sapare wa Gyɛruzɛlɛm,
translated: When King Herod heard this, he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him. When he had called together all the people’s chief priests and teachers of the law, he asked them where the Messiah was to be born. “In Bethlehem in Judea,” they replied, “for this is what the prophet has written:

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Dabeɛ̃ ne nɔmaa da kpɛ ba la yaga zaa, ka ba soorɔ taa, “Aŋ mɔɔ la a neɛ ŋa, haali ka saseɛ ane a mane sagra O noɔre?”
translated: The hare and the sheep met on the road, and the hare asked, "Is this how you are, even though you have such a big tail O sheep?"

ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa woŋ a Gyɔɔn kũũ yɛlɛ, O da yi la be kpɛ gbori gaa zie kaŋ a te bebe O yoŋ. Kyɛ a nembeɛoŋ naŋ da wa woŋ O yɛlɛ, ba da yi la a tenne poore a kyɛŋ beɛre gaa O zie.
translated: On the day that he was going to Accra, he said he would return and buy a bicycle for his younger sibling. But on the day that he was coming back, he fell from the vehicle and broke his leg.

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Lɛ la ka a Yuzɛb iri de a bie ane O ma yi ne tensogɔ gaa ne a Igyipiti teŋɛ,
translated: Then Joseph got up, took the child and his mother, and went to the land of Egypt.
